testcase.name: test_perform_stream_request_failure
testcase.failure:

testcase.name: test_perform_stream_request_failure

failure.message: TypeError: 'SSEClient' object is not an iterator

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.llms.test_mistral_client.TestMistralClientStreaming testMethod=test_perform_stream_request_failure> mock_api_key = <MagicMock name='api_key' id='4375920528'> mock_post = <MagicMock name='post' id='4375978832'>
@patch("tinychat.llms.mistral.treq.post") # Updated to treq.post @patch("tinychat.llms.base.BaseLLMClient.api_key", new_callable=MagicMock) def test_perform_stream_request_failure(self, mock_api_key, mock_post): # Setting a dummy value for mock_api_key is not strictly needed here
# Setup: Mocking the response with an error status code mock_response = Mock() mock_response.status_code = 400 mock_response.json = Mock(return_value={}) # Added to simulate treq response mock_post.return_value = mock_response
# Execution client = MistralClient(model_name="test_model", temperature = 0.0) messages = [{"role": "user", "content": "hello"}]
# Verification: Expecting a ValueError on error response with self.assertRaises(ValueError) as context: > next(client.perform_stream_request(messages)) # type: ignore ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E TypeError: 'SSEClient' object is not an iterator
tests/llms/test_mistral_client.py:68: TypeError
